static_findings.description:
WalletDelegate.executeInLibrary(bytes) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#43-52) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#47-49)

WalletDelegate.executeInLibrary(bytes) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#43-52) uses delegatecall to a input-controlled function id
	- (ok,ret) = libraryAddr.delegatecall(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#45)
WalletDelegate.owner (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#17) should be immutable

Version constraint ^0.8.0 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess
	- AbiReencodingHeadOverflowWithStaticArrayCleanup
	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage
	- DataLocationChangeInInternalOverride
	- NestedCalldataArrayAbiReencodingSizeValidation
	- SignedImmutables
	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory
	- KeccakCaching.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#8)

Low level call in WalletDelegate.executeWithPermit(address,bytes,uint256,uint256,WalletDelegate.Signature) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#77-95):
	- (ok,ret) = target.call{value: value}(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#92)

Low level call in WalletDelegate.executeOnTarget(address,bytes,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#54-59):
	- (ok,ret) = target.call{value: value}(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#55)

Low level call in WalletDelegate.executeInLibrary(bytes) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#43-52):
	- (ok,ret) = libraryAddr.delegatecall(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#45)

Low level call in WalletDelegate.batchExecute(address[],bytes[],uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#61-75):
	- (ok,ret) = targets[i].call{value: values[i]}(calldatas[i]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#70)

Low level call in WalletDelegate.withdraw(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#102-107):
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#105)

WalletDelegate.executeOnTarget(address,bytes,uint256).target (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#54) lacks a zero-check on :
		- (ok,ret) = target.call{value: value}(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#55)

WalletDelegate.executeWithPermit(address,bytes,uint256,uint256,WalletDelegate.Signature).target (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#78) lacks a zero-check on :
		- (ok,ret) = target.call{value: value}(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#92)

WalletDelegate.batchExecute(address[],bytes[],uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#61-75) has external calls inside a loop: (ok,ret) = targets[i].call{value: values[i]}(calldatas[i]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#70)

Reentrancy in WalletDelegate.executeOnTarget(address,bytes,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#54-59):
	External calls:
	- (ok,ret) = target.call{value: value}(data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#55)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- Executed(target,data) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#57)

WalletDelegate.executeWithPermit(address,bytes,uint256,uint256,WalletDelegate.Signature) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#77-95) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(block.timestamp <= deadline,permit expired) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#84)
Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.

The `ecrecover` function is susceptible to signature malleability. This means that the same message can be signed in multiple ways, allowing an attacker to change the message signature without invalidating it. This can lead to unexpected behavior in smart contracts, such as the loss of funds or the ability to bypass access control. Consider using OpenZeppelin's ECDSA library instead of the built-in function.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.

Avoid `require` / `revert` statements in a loop because a single bad item can cause the whole transaction to fail. It's better to forgive on fail and return failed elements post processing of the loop
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
State variables that are only changed in the constructor should be declared immutable to save gas. Add the `immutable` attribute to state variables that are only changed in the constructor
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`

	- INLINE ASM (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_qqkrp2w9.sol#47-49)
